task_completion.description: Webots 3D scene shows the arena, walls, and robot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots 3D viewport displays a floor (RectangleArena), four box-like walls forming an enclosure or corridors, and a Pioneer 3-AT robot (a red, 4-wheeled robot).

task_completion.description: Webots scene tree contains the required nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots scene tree panel (usually on the left) lists 'WorldInfo', 'RectangleArena', multiple 'Solid' nodes (ideally with DEF names like WALL_N, WALL_S, WALL_E, WALL_W), and a 'Pioneer3at' node (ideally DEF SLAM_ROBOT).

task_completion.description: Robot is equipped with required sensors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The expanded Pioneer3at node in the scene tree (e.g., in the extensionSlot) shows the presence of Lidar ('scanner'), GPS ('gps'), InertialUnit ('imu'), and PositionSensors ('left_encoder', 'right_encoder'), OR the controller code shows these devices being retrieved via getDevice().

task_completion.description: Controller script initializes sensors and logs to CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An open code editor shows 'slam_logger.py' with code to initialize sensors (Lidar, GPS, IMU, encoders), a simulation loop ('while robot.step'), and file writing operations appending data to '/tmp/slam_log.csv'.

task_completion.description: Controller script sets wheel motor velocities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor showing 'slam_logger.py' includes motor initialization and calls to set the velocity of both wheel motors to 3.0.

task_completion.description: World and controller files are saved in the correct locations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots window title bar, a file manager, or a terminal confirms the world is saved as 'slam_benchmark.wbt' on the Desktop, and the controller is saved in the 'slam_logger' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Webots GUI to build or verify the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Webots application interface is visible in the screenshots, showing the 3D viewport and scene tree, rather than just a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor or IDE to write the controller script.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., VS Code, Nano, Vim, or Webots built-in editor) is visible showing the Python code, rather than the code being generated via a single terminal echo command.

integrity.description: The agent built the world from scratch as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen adding nodes to an empty world or editing the world properties, rather than loading a fully complete pre-existing world file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The world file must be saved at '/home/ga/Desktop/slam_benchmark.wbt'. The controller script must be saved at '/home/ga/webots_projects/slam_bench/controllers/slam_logger/slam_logger.py'. The Webots scene must contain a RectangleArena, 4 Solid nodes for walls (DEF WALL_N, WALL_S, WALL_E, WALL_W), and a Pioneer3at robot (DEF SLAM_ROBOT). The controller must log data to '/tmp/slam_log.csv' and set motor velocities to 3.0.
